name: Jacksonville to New York LTL
hero-headline: Jacksonville, FL to New York, NY LTL Freight
subheadline: 986 miles. 3-4 business day transit. 5 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Jacksonville to New York

The Jacksonville to New York LTL lane covers approximately 986 miles between the Southeast and Northeast regions. Standard transit runs 3-4 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Atlanta Hub, Miami Gateway, Charlotte Terminal support consolidation and transit optimization. Hurricane risk Jun-Nov. Consistent retail demand year-round.

How WARP Operates the Jacksonville to New York Lane

WARP covers the Jacksonville to New York corridor with 5 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 3-4 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Atlanta Hub and Miami Gateway enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 986 miles (Jacksonville to New York)
  • Transit window: 3-4 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 5
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Atlanta Hub, Miami Gateway, Charlotte Terminal, NY/NJ Metro Hub, Philadelphia Terminal
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Jacksonville to New York lane range from $3,279 to $4,371, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 986 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $3.
  • 88/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Jacksonville to New York LTL (986-mile corridor; 3–4 day transit window; 5 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Atlanta Hub and Miami Gateway). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Jacksonville, FL to New York, NY cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $3,279 to $4,371 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Jacksonville to New York?

Standard LTL transit on this 986-mile lane is 3-4 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Jacksonville to New York lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 5 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Jacksonville to New York in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Jacksonville to New York corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
